Trainer who first defeats all of their opponent’s Pokémon.

In Double Battles, you and an opponent will each send out
two Pokémon at a time to do battle. Victory will go to the
Trainer who first defeats all of their opponent’s Pokémon.

want to learn how to tailor your strategy to this format.

and their types or Abilities may also change.

given move will be affected by how the move’s type matches up
with the type or types of the target Pokémon.

type matchup.

super effective, not very effective, and has no effect.

matchup for each of the Pokémon’s types will be combined
to determine the final matchup between that Pokémon and
the move used on it. This can lead to the following two
matchups: extremely effective and mostly ineffective.

mSDataSet.OriginalText: Time Limits

There are a variety of different time limits that come into play
when battling. If you don’t make a decision in time, an action
will automatically be decided for you to keep the battle moving.

when battling. If you don’t make a decision in time, an action

mSDataSet.OriginalText:
Total Time

This is the total time available for the entire battle.

If this time runs out, the battle will end, and the result will be a
draw if you’re having a Casual Battle or Ranked Battle.
A draw doesn’t award Victory Points (VP) or Season Points (SP),
and your rank does not change.

by effects from sources such as moves, Abilities, and held items.

Stat changes are measured in stages ranging from −6 to +6.
The negative numbers indicate that a stat has been lowered,
while the positive numbers indicate that it has been boosted.

When a Pokémon is switched out of battle, these numbers
will be reset to zero.

When your Pokémon’s accuracy is lowered, it will have a harder
time hitting targets with its moves. When its accuracy is boosted,

at the end of every turn.

The Pokémon takes increasing poison damage at the end of
every turn, starting at 1/16 of the Pokémon’s max HP, then
2/16, then 3/16, and so on. If the Pokémon switches out and
reenters battle, the damage restarts at 1/16 of its max HP.

mSDataSet.OriginalText:
Paralyzed Status Condition

The Pokémon has a 12.5% chance of being unable to use
moves. Its Speed stat is also halved.

TextInfos.Text: The Pokémon has a 12.5% chance of being unable to use
moves. Its Speed stat is also halved.

mSDataSet.OriginalText:
Frozen Status Condition

The Pokémon can’t use almost any moves but has a 25%
chance of being cured each time it tries to do so. The
Pokémon will be cured on its third turn after becoming
frozen or if it takes damage from a Fire-type move or
certain other moves.

mSDataSet.OriginalText:
Asleep Status Condition

The Pokémon can’t use moves other than Snore or
Sleep Talk. After a Pokémon is put to sleep, it will
remain asleep on its first turn, it will have a 1-in-3
chance of being cured on its second turn, and it will be
cured on its third turn. A Pokémon that used the move
Rest has no chance of being cured until its third turn.

TextInfos.Text: The Pokémon can’t use moves other than Snore or
Sleep Talk. After a Pokémon is put to sleep, it will
remain asleep on its first turn, it will have a 1-in-3
chance of being cured on its second turn, and it will be
cured on its third turn. A Pokémon that used the move
Rest has no chance of being cured until its third turn.

mSDataSet.OriginalText: Contact

Moves can be broadly divided into two groups:
contact moves, which make contact with the target, and
noncontact moves, which don’t make contact with the target.

There are a variety of moves, held items, and Abilities that can
either affect contact moves or are triggered by contact moves.

Let’s look at some examples.

either affect contact moves or are triggered by contact moves.

mSDataSet.OriginalText:
King’s Shield

The user protects itself from incoming attacks for the turn and
changes into its Shield Forme. Any attackers that use contact
moves against the user during this turn will have their Attack
stats lowered by 1 stage. With each consecutive use, this
move’s chance of success becomes 1/3 of what it was before.
